requirements.text: Capture this owner directive verbatim via /directive-compliance before any other action.
requirements.required_evidence: project-control/directives/D-004-agent-teams-runtime-adoption/source-001.md with recorded sha256

requirements.text: D-003 integration of PR #100 and PR #101 is complete; do not reopen it.
Perform the start-of-session routine: run python tools/project_control.py status; read SESSION_HANDOFF.md; reconcile the ledger against git and CI; confirm main is clean.
requirements.required_evidence: status output, handoff read, origin/main vs local SHA comparison, CI conclusions, git status

requirements.text: Verify the CLI supports agent teams (v2.1.178 or later, where teammates spawn with no setup step).
requirements.required_evidence: claude --version output

requirements.text: Verify the CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS env flag is active in THIS session.
requirements.required_evidence: environment read showing the flag value in this session

requirements.text: If the CLI does not support agent teams or the env flag is not active in this session, STOP and tell the owner to relaunch with it exported.
Persistence: if .claude/settings.local.json exists, READ it and MERGE {"env":{"CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS":"1"}} into it without altering unrelated keys; create the file only if absent.
requirements.required_evidence: pre/post key inventory of settings.local.json proving unrelated keys unchanged

requirements.text: Run `git check-ignore .claude/settings.local.json` and show the output.
requirements.required_evidence: verbatim git check-ignore -v output

requirements.text: If .claude/settings.local.json is not git-ignored, STOP and report before writing anything.
The ledger, git, and CI are the sole truth for all phases of this directive.
The runtime team task list holds only ledger task ID references (never substantive state).
ADR-005 is unchanged: the orchestrator alone runs project_control.py, git, and gh.
The orchestrator performs every rebase and every merge.
Producers never push, never run gh, and never rebase.
After the orchestrator rebases, the producer only re-runs checks, and supplies content resolutions in its own worktree if a conflict lands in its scoped files.
Sequential integration: one PR at a time, reconcile main, and re-run the required gates before the next.
Reviewers are spawned ONLY from named .claude/agents/ definitions, so that agent_type-gated guards recognize them.
requirements.required_evidence: spawn records naming the .claude/agents/ definition used for each reviewer

requirements.text: Spawn only the reviewers the content requires.
Each reviewer spawn prompt states the frozen 40-character SHA under review.
requirements.required_evidence: spawn prompt text containing the 40-char SHA

requirements.text: Each reviewer spawn prompt explicitly instructs /run-quality-gate, because skills frontmatter does not auto-apply to teammates.
requirements.required_evidence: spawn prompt text containing the explicit /run-quality-gate instruction

requirements.text: Every tracked file this directive produces (including pilot reports) must belong to a contracted task whose allowed_paths cover it; nothing floats outside a task.
requirements.required_evidence: task packet allowed_paths covering every tracked file produced under D-004

requirements.text: Do NOT touch M0-T025 in any way.
Evidence hygiene (public repository): record only teammate names and agent types taken from team configuration.
Redact session IDs, pane IDs, absolute user paths, and all machine-specific data from anything written to the repository.
requirements.required_evidence: redaction check over every D-004 report file before commit

requirements.text: /resume does not restore teammates: every new conversation spawns a fresh team; never message a prior team.
After ANY hook or settings file change is merged, testing of it happens only in a completely fresh Claude session; never assume a running session reloaded it.
STEP 1 begins only on the owner's explicit GO.
Before the Step 1 pilot, contract a pilot-governance control task with its own ID through the normal packet flow.
The pilot-governance task's allowed_paths are exactly the named pilot report files plus its own producer report.
requirements.required_evidence: task packet allowed_paths list

requirements.text: All pilot evidence lives under the pilot-governance task.
Target one frozen, already-accepted SHA (for example the post-integration main head) for the reviewer pilot.
requirements.required_evidence: the frozen 40-char SHA recorded in the pilot report

requirements.text: The Step 1 pilot is mechanism proof only; the ledger's product state is untouched.
Spawn 2 to 3 reviewer teammates from named definitions appropriate to that SHA's content.
Record in project-control/reports/AGENT-TEAMS-PILOT-1.md (redacted) each teammate's own git rev-parse output showing the reviewed SHA.
requirements.required_evidence: per-teammate git rev-parse output in the pilot-1 report

requirements.text: Record teammate names and agent types only, taken from team configuration, in the pilot-1 report.
Negative test: one reviewer attempts to create a disposable sentinel using BOTH a Write-tool attempt AND a Bash redirection such as `echo x > ./PILOT_SENTINEL.tmp`.
requirements.required_harness: reviewer-side Write attempt + Bash redirection attempt against ./PILOT_SENTINEL.tmp

requirements.text: Capture the guard denials from the negative test verbatim in the pilot-1 report.
requirements.required_evidence: verbatim denial text for both the Write attempt and the Bash redirection attempt

requirements.text: The orchestrator independently verifies with `test -e` that the sentinel was never created, and records that verification in the pilot-1 report.
requirements.required_evidence: orchestrator-run `test -e` result recorded verbatim

requirements.text: Confirm in the pilot-1 report that each reviewer actually invoked /run-quality-gate.
Preserve verbatim the reviewers' verdicts and full report content delivered to the orchestrator.
STOP after Step 1 and present the evidence to the owner.
STEP 2 begins only on the owner's explicit GO.
The orchestrator pre-creates two worktrees and two branches from a frozen base SHA for the probe.
Spawn two teammates with strictly read-only instructions: no Write or Edit, and Bash limited to pwd and git inspection.
Give each probe teammate its EXPECTED_WORKTREE, EXPECTED_BRANCH, and EXPECTED_BASE_SHA.
Each probe teammate must attest twice - once on arrival and once after at least one further turn - returning pwd, git rev-parse --show-toplevel, git branch --show-current, and git rev-parse HEAD.
requirements.required_harness: two attestations per teammate, each returning the four values

requirements.text: Success for the probe requires both teammates to match their DISTINCT assigned worktrees on BOTH attestations.
Record probe results (redacted) in project-control/reports/AGENT-TEAMS-PILOT-2-PROBE.md under the governance task.
Tear down the probe worktrees after the probe.
If the probe FAILS: STOP; producers stay on the existing default (background subagents with isolation: worktree); only review waves use teams; skip Steps 3 and 4 and tell the owner.
STEP 3 begins only on the owner's explicit GO and only if Step 2 passed.
Create a NEW producer-confinement task with its own ID, scope, acceptance tests, and independent reviewers.
Never touch or repurpose M0-T025 for the producer-confinement task.
Guarantee model: best-effort prevention plus guaranteed detection before integration; never claim out-of-scope writes are impossible.
Confinement layer 1: bind session to task ID to worktree to branch to frozen base SHA; refuse producer work when the binding is missing or ambiguous.
Confinement layer 2: a PreToolUse hook blocks Write/Edit destinations outside allowed_paths.
Confinement layer 3: a PreToolUse hook blocks obvious repository-mutating Bash outside the assigned worktree.
Confinement layer 4: run a containment diff on BOTH TaskCompleted AND TeammateIdle, because task status can lag.
The containment diff compares against the frozen base and covers committed, staged, unstaged, renamed (BOTH sides of every rename must be allowed), deleted, and non-ignored untracked files.
The containment diff refuses completion on any escape.

Confinement layer 5 (residual): worktree isolation plus the orchestrator's final diff review before integration, since interpreter and test-runner writes cannot be statically blocked and layers 4 and 5 catch them.
Acceptance tests for the confinement task cover Windows paths, "..", symlinks, absolute paths, mixed-case paths, shell redirects, git -C, generated files, and scripting-language writes.
requirements.required_harness: executable acceptance tests for each of the nine named escape vectors

requirements.text: STOP after the producer-confinement task's gate passes.
STEP 4 begins only on the owner's explicit GO.
Start a COMPLETELY FRESH Claude session before Step 4 so the merged hooks load.
Run two producer teammates on genuinely disjoint, already-contracted second-wave tasks.
The orchestrator pre-creates worktrees and branches from a frozen base for the two producers.
Record the ORCHESTRATION_POLICY section C matrix: task ID, branch, worktree, allowed and forbidden paths, expected shared files (none), and merge order.
Mandatory attestation before any write: compare EXPECTED_WORKTREE, EXPECTED_BRANCH, and EXPECTED_BASE_SHA against the four returned values (pwd, toplevel, current branch, HEAD).
requirements.required_harness: pre-write attestation of the four values against the three expected values

requirements.text: On any attestation mismatch, stop that producer and report.
Producers stay inside allowed_paths and return evidence plus a requested status of awaiting_gate, blocked, or needs_split.
The orchestrator performs all git integration for the two-producer pilot.
Record Step 4 evidence (redacted) in project-control/reports/AGENT-TEAMS-PILOT-3.md under the governance task.
STOP after Step 4 and present the evidence to the owner.
STEP 5 begins only on the owner's explicit GO after Step 4 passes.
Contract a final adoption task for the Step 5 amendments.
Amend ORCHESTRATION_POLICY section 1: teams are the preferred wave runtime when the session supports them; background subagents remain the fallback.
Amend ORCHESTRATION_POLICY section 1a: the task list persists locally; /resume does not restore teammates; a fresh team is spawned every conversation; the ledger is authoritative.
Amend ORCHESTRATION_POLICY section C: mandatory producer worktree attestation plus reviewer spawn rules become permanent procedure.
Move the agent-teams env flag into tracked .claude/settings.json.
Keep teammateDefaultModel at "leader's model" via /config ONLY; it lives as teammateDefaultModel: null in the global ~/.claude.json and must never appear in any tracked project settings file.
Present exact diffs before committing the Step 5 changes.
Route the Step 5 changes through independent review.

From adoption onward, standard waves use at most 3 producers, packet-applicable reviewers in parallel on frozen SHAs, sequential merges, and the orchestrator rebasing surviving branches after each merge while producers re-run checks in parallel.
AT EVERY STOP: present the evidence and the exact proposed diffs to the owner, then wait for the owner's explicit GO.
Anything ambiguous, blocked, or hold-releasing becomes a blocker, not an action.

Record this direction as an append-only amendment: source-002-amendment.md, new requirement IDs starting at D-004-R087, a manifest.amendments[] entry, recomputed digests, and no edits to existing requirement rows.

requirements.id_correction_note: The owner's amendment text cites D-004-R083. D-004-R083 reads 'Route the Step 5 changes through independent review' and carries neither a value clause nor a prohibition clause. The two clauses quoted verbatim by the owner ('teammateDefaultModel ... null ... leader's model' and 'never in any tracked project settings file') are both verbatim D-004-R081. The supersession is therefore recorded against D-004-R081 and the discrepancy is raised to the owner as D-004-R095. D-004-R083 is untouched.

requirements.text: The prohibition clause of the superseded requirement is RE-AFFIRMED UNCHANGED: teammateDefaultModel must never appear in any tracked project settings file.
